description_long: This object is a solar energy infrastructure installation featuring a prominent rectangular array of photovoltaic panels mounted atop a robust, industrial base. The solar panels are arranged in a single, slightly tilted row, supported by a metallic frame structure. The base is painted yellow and gray, with visible mechanical and electrical components, including what appear to be access panels and control units. The structure is designed for ground installation, likely intended for urban or roadside environments, and is built to withstand outdoor conditions. The overall design suggests durability and functionality, with the solar array elevated to maximize sunlight exposure. No visible cabling or inverter housing is apparent from the exterior views.
description: A ground-mounted solar energy installation with a tilted rectangular solar panel array on a sturdy yellow and gray base, featuring industrial details and robust construction.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the solar panel array and the detailed yellow and gray base structure.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the base and the tilt of the solar panels, with visible mechanical details.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the solar panel array and the base, with less visible detail on the control units.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the other end of the base and the panel tilt, mirroring view 1.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 25 degrees, azimuth direction not clearly discernible, orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted with a metallic (likely steel or aluminum) frame and industrial base
attribute_array_configuration: single linear row of approximately 6 panels
